creator: vsjcfm@gmail.com
text:
2013/6/12 Christoph Theis <theis@gmx.at>:
> Hello!
>
> Am 12.06.2013 15:07, schrieb Sayetsky Anton:
>
>> Action file for IPFW now contains "actionstart" (creating blocking
>> rule) & "actionstop" (deleting blocking rule). I think that's
>> incorrect because in this setup number of blocking rule is determined
>> by fail2bam itself. I think that blocking rule must add firewall
>> administrator only.
>
>
> I agree that the patch files/patch-pf.conf for pf.conf is no longer
> necessary. Also that the patch files/patch-bsd-ipfw.conf is basically
> the same as config/action.d/bsd-ipfw.conf. So we may remove both patches
> from files/...
>
> There is an ongoing discussion on the fail2ban mailing list regarding a
> possible DOS attack via fail2ban and I intend to cleanup those two files
> when I submit a patch for that.
>
> But I don't agree with your concern regarding actionstart and actionstop:
> When fail2ban blocks an IP address, fail2ban will add a rule to the firewall
> to block that IP address. It might do that by adding a table to ipfw and add
> / remove IP addresses to this table, or it might add that IP address
> directly to ipfw with a blocking rule.
>
> I would like to leave bsd-ipfw.conf as is. If the firewall administrator is
> not the one configuring fail2ban you might need your own bsd-ipfw.local
> anyway.
>
>
> Christoph
>
>
I've attached to this email new patch that removes files/patch-pf.conf
& files/patch-bsd-ipfw.conf.
If you think that it's correct, please approve.
